perturbation.original_text: Attendee will preserve as confidential all Confidential Information that Attendee may obtain or prepare in relation to the Test during the Testing Period. Without BOMI’s prior written consent, which may be given or withheld by BOMI in its sole and absolute discretion, Attendee will not at any time, whether during or after the Testing Period, (a) disclose any Confidential Information to any third party nor gi ve any third party access thereto, nor (b) use any Confidential Information except as required in taking the Test.
perturbation.changed_text: Attendee will endeavor to preserve as confidential all Confidential Information that Attendee may obtain or prepare in relation to the Test during the Testing Period. Without BOMI’s prior written consent, which may be given or withheld by BOMI, Attendee will not generally disclose any Confidential Information to any third party nor give any third party routine access thereto, nor (b) use Confidential Information unless generally permissible.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will preserve as confidential' to 'will endeavor to preserve as confidential' and 'will not at any time disclose' to 'will not generally disclose' weakens the obligation. Also, 'except as required in taking the Test' to 'unless generally permissible' is far too open to interpretation.
perturbation.law_explanation: The modification allows for ambiguous and potentially broad exceptions to non-disclosure, contradicting the MUTSA, which aims to protect trade secrets (which confidential test information could be considered) through clear prohibitions on unauthorized disclosure and use. The 'endeavor' and 'generally permissible' language creates loopholes that undermine the reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy required for trade secret protection.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1201 (2022) - Definitions :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Commercial Law Title 11 - Trade Regulation Subtitle 12 - Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act Section 11-1201 - Definitions Universal Citation: MD Commercial Law Code § 11-1201 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Next (a) In this subtitle the following words have the meanings indicated. (b) “Improper means” includes theft, bribery, misrepresentation, breach or inducement of a breach of a duty to maintain secrecy, or espionage through electronic or other means. (c) “Misappropriation” means the: (1) Acquisition of a trade secret of another by a person who knows or has reason to know that the trade secret was acquired by improper means; or (2) Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent by a person who: (i) Used improper means to acquire knowledge of the trade secret; or (ii) At the time of disclosure or use, knew or had reason to know that the person’s knowledge of the trade secret was: 1. Derived from or through a person who had utilized improper means to acquire it; 2. Acquired under circumstances giving rise to a duty to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or 3. Derived from or through a person who owed a duty to the person seeking relief to maintain its secrecy or limit its use; or (iii) Before a material change of the person’s position, knew or had reason to know that it was a trade secret and that knowledge of it had been acquired by accident or mistake. (d) “Person” means an individual, corporation, business trust, statutory trust, estate, trust, partnership, association, joint venture, government, governmental subdivision or a
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to preserve confidentiality and introduces ambiguity, potentially contradicting the MUTSA's aim to protect trade secrets. By changing 'will preserve as confidential' to 'will endeavor to preserve as confidential' and 'will not at any time disclose' to 'will not generally disclose', the contract now allows for broader exceptions to non-disclosure. The scraped law snippet from Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1201 defines 'Misappropriation' as the 'Disclosure or use of a trade secret of another without express or implied consent,' and the modified text creates loopholes that could undermine reasonable efforts to maintain secrecy, thereby contradicting the law's intent. The phrase 'unless generally permissible' also opens the door for subjective interpretations and potential misuse of confidential information, further weakening the original obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Attendee shall notify BO MI immediately upon discovery of unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement, and shall cooperate with BOMI in every reasonable way to assist BOMI in mitigating the consequences of such use or disclosure and preven ting further unauthorized use or disclosu re.
perturbation.changed_text: Attendee should consider notifying BOMI upon discovery of unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any breach of this Agreement, and should attempt to cooperate with BOMI to assist BOMI in mitigating the consequences of such use or disclosure and preventing further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall notify' to 'should consider notifying' and 'shall cooperate' to 'should attempt to cooperate' makes the obligation to report and assist discretionary, not mandatory.
perturbation.law_explanation: Under the Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act, failing to report or assist in mitigating unauthorized disclosure (which the changes now make optional) can hinder BOMI's ability to pursue legal remedies or prevent further damage. Section 11-1205 pertains to injunctive relief for misappropriation. If BOMI isn't made aware of a breach, its ability to seek timely injunctive relief is compromised.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1205 (2022) - Preservation of Trade Secret During Action :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Commercial Law Title 11 - Trade Regulation Subtitle 12 - Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act Section 11-1205 - Preservation of Trade Secret During Action Universal Citation: MD Commercial Law Code § 11-1205 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous Next In an action under this subtitle, a court shall preserve the secrecy of an alleged trade secret by reasonable means, which may include granting protective orders in connection with discovery proceedings, holding in-camera hearings, sealing the records of the action, and ordering any person involved in the litigation not to disclose an alleged trade secret without prior court approval. perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall notify' to 'should consider notifying' and 'shall cooperate' to 'should attempt to cooperate' transforms mandatory actions into discretionary ones. The Law Explanation states that this weakens BOMI's ability to seek timely injunctive relief under the Maryland Uniform Trade Secrets Act, particularly concerning misappropriation, as prompt reporting and assistance are compromised. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing Maryland Commercial Law Code Section 11-1205, addresses the preservation of trade secrets during legal action. By making notification and cooperation discretionary, the changed text directly undermines BOMI's ability to promptly address and mitigate any damage. This contradicts the intent of the law to preserve trade secrets through timely action.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of any litigation between the Parties in connection with this Agreement, including the seeking of injunctive relief, if BOMI is the successful party, Attendee shall pay to BOMI therein all co sts and expenses, including but not limited to actual attorneys’ fees incurred therein by BOMI, which costs, expens es and attorneys’ fees shall be included as a 2 part of any judgment rendered in such action in addition to a ny other relief to which BOMI may be entitled.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of any litigation between the Parties in connection with this Agreement, including the seeking of injunctive relief, should BOMI be considered the prevailing party, Attendee may be responsible for a portion of BOMI’s reasonable costs and expenses, including attorneys’ fees, subject to court discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'if BOMI is the successful party' to 'should BOMI be considered the prevailing party' introduces subjectivity. Replacing 'Attendee shall pay to BOMI therein all costs and expenses' with 'Attendee may be responsible for a portion of BOMI’s reasonable costs and expenses' significantly weakens the obligation and makes the amount discretionary, particularly with 'subject to court discretion'.
perturbation.law_explanation: While Maryland Rule 2-701 does provide for judgements for costs, the original text explicitly states that the Attendee *shall* pay all costs, including attorney fees if BOMI is successful. The modified text makes cost recovery discretionary ('may be responsible') and limits it to a 'portion' of 'reasonable' costs subject to court discretion. This ambiguity and shift away from the strict language of the original creates ambiguity about the allocation of costs and attorney's fees and can be interpreted to imply an agreement to bear one's own fees in situations where that isn't explicitly stated in a judgment for costs.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Message - Maryland Code and Court Rules This document is not available on Westlaw.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Attendee to pay all costs if BOMI wins. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating the Attendee 'may be responsible for a portion' of costs, subject to court discretion. This directly contradicts the original, creating an Ambiguity Legal Contradiction. The 'Perturbation Explanation' confirms this weakens the obligation. The 'Law Explanation' states this weakens the original, and shifts the allocation of costs creating ambiguity. The change shifts the responsibility from a definitive obligation to a discretionary one, thus creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by and c onstrued according to the laws of the State of Maryland (excluding the choice of laws ru les thereof). Attendee hereby consents to the jurisdiction of the courts of the State of Maryland. Any su it brought hereon must be brought in the state or federal courts sitti ng in the State of Maryland, the parties hereto hereby waive any claim or defense that such foru m is not convenient or proper.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be generally guided by the laws of the State of Maryland. Attendee consents to the possibility of jurisdiction in the courts of the State of Maryland. Any suit brought hereon should ideally be brought in the state or federal courts sitting in the State of Maryland, the parties hereto may consider waiving any claim or defense that such forum is not convenient or proper.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'will be governed by and construed according to the laws' to 'will be generally guided by the laws' weakens the governing law provision. Changing 'Attendee hereby consents to the jurisdiction' to 'Attendee consents to the possibility of jurisdiction' and 'Any suit brought hereon must be brought' to 'Any suit brought hereon should ideally be brought' significantly weakens the forum selection clause. Replacing 'hereby waive' with 'may consider waiving' makes the waiver non-binding.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Maryland Code, Courts and Judicial Proceedings, § 6-104
perturbation.law_citation: MD Code, Courts and Judicial Proceedings, § 6-104
perturbation.law_explanation: Maryland law recognizes the validity of forum selection clauses and waivers of objections to venue. By weakening the forum selection clause and the waiver ('should ideally be brought', 'may consider waiving'), the modification introduces ambiguity that could undermine the enforceability of these provisions, potentially conflicting with the spirit and purpose of MD Code, Courts and Judicial Proceedings, § 6-104.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Maryland Courts and Judicial Proceedings Code Section 6-104 (2022) - Stay or Dismissal of Action Which Should Be Heard in Another Forum; Consolidation of Actions in District Court and Circuit Court :: 2022 Maryland Code :: U.S. Codes and Statutes :: U.S. Law :: Justia There Is a Newer Version of this Section 2024 Maryland Code 2023 Maryland Code 2022 Maryland Code (here) View All Versions View Our Newest Version Here 2022 Maryland Statutes Courts and Judicial Proceedings Title 6 - Personal Jurisdiction, Venue, Process and Practice Subtitle 1 - Bases of Personal Jurisdiction Section 6-104 - Stay or Dismissal of Action Which Should Be Heard in Another Forum; Consolidation of Actions in District Court and Circuit Court Universal Citation: MD Courts and Judicial Proceedings Code § 6-104 (2022) Learn more This media-neutral citation is based on the American Association of Law Libraries Universal Citation Guide and is not necessarily the official citation. Previous (a) If a court finds that in the interest of substantial justice an action should be heard in another forum, the court may stay or dismiss the action in whole or in part on any conditions it considers just. (b) (1) When actions involving at least one of the same parties, with the same subject matter, issues, and defenses arising out of the same circumstances, are instituted in the District Court and a circuit court of the State, the party who filed the action in the District Court may file a motion in that court to remove the action to the circuit court for consolidation with the action pending in circuit court. (2) If the motion is granted, the court shall forward all pleadings to the circuit court. (3) An action removed to the circuit court and consolidated with an action in that court under the provisions of this section shall proceed in the circuit court, without regard to the amount in controversy, as if originally instituted in the circuit court. Previous Disclaimer: These codes may not be the most recent version. Maryland may have more current or accurate inform
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a legal contradiction by introducing ambiguity into provisions that were previously definitive, undermining their enforceability under Maryland law. The original contract stated the agreement 'will be governed by and construed according to the laws,' whereas the revised contract says the agreement will be 'generally guided by the laws,' which weakens the governing law provision. The original text states 'Attendee hereby consents to the jurisdiction,' whereas the revised text states 'Attendee consents to the possibility of jurisdiction,' creating ambiguity regarding consent. Similarly, 'Any suit brought hereon must be brought' is weakened to 'Any suit brought hereon should ideally be brought.' Finally, 'hereby waive' is changed to 'may consider waiving,' creating ambiguity and a non-binding waiver. The Law Explanation supports that these changes potentially conflict with the spirit and purpose of MD Code, Courts and Judicial Proceedings, § 6-104, by weakening the forum selection clause and waiver. MD Courts and Judicial Proceedings Code § 6-104 addresses actions being heard in another forum, implying a legal expectation of clear jurisdictional agreements. By making the forum selection and waiver ambiguous, the changed contract introduces a contradiction.
